questions.description: The plan is already chunked. This decides whether the generated goal asks an agent to complete the whole roadmap or only the first independently valid milestone.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Execute the full plan as a sequence of chunk stop points, starting at Chunk A and stopping after each validated chunk unless the /goal runner is explicitly told to continue.

questions.prompt: How should Electron-targeted page assertions be handled in the initial implementation?
questions.description: The current bridge routes Electron targets through a separate server path. Implementing full Electron assertions is possible but larger than the browser-tab CI path.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Keep Electron page assertions out of scope for the initial implementation, but add a tested explicit unsupported error so --target electron assertions fail clearly.

questions.prompt: Should native ref actions support iframe/subframe refs in the first pass?
questions.description: Current native input evaluates selectors in the main tab context. Real subframe support needs frame-aware CDP context and coordinate handling.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Defer subframe native support. In the first pass, native ref actions should support main-frame refs and fail clearly for subframe refs.

questions.prompt: What live browser validation should be required before this goal is done?
questions.description: Unit and integration tests can cover most logic. Live extension/browser coverage is heavier and belongs in Playwright e2e or documented manual smoke.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Require a deterministic local-fixture Playwright extension e2e smoke when stable; if it is blocked or flaky, document the exact blocker and keep manual smoke commands in docs.
